title: Basic
summary: Defines multilinear ambient inputs/operators and basic operations for replacing or transforming input slots.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines multilinear ambient inputs/operators and basic operations for replacing or transforming input slots.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows PolyGJMSoperators/Basic.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A rank-r tuple of ambient functions. In explicit terms, for calconf of type calculus conf, and r in the natural numbers, its defining expression is Fin r maps to Function Conf.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev MultiInput (_CalConf : Calculus Conf) (r : ℕ) : Type _

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A rank-r ambient operator. In explicit terms, for calconf of type calculus conf, and r in the natural numbers, its defining expression is Cal Conf.Multi Input r maps to Function Conf.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev MultiOperator (CalConf : Calculus Conf) (r : ℕ) : Type _

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Rank-one multi-operators. In explicit terms, its defining expression is Cal Conf.Multi Operator 1.
Rank-two multi-operators, i.e. bidifferential ambient operators. In explicit terms, its defining expression is Cal Conf.Multi Operator 2.
Rank-three multi-operators, i.e. tridifferential ambient operators. In explicit terms, its defining expression is Cal Conf.Multi Operator 3.

Regard an ordinary unary ambient operator as a rank-one multi-operator. In explicit terms, for p of type function conf maps to function conf, its defining expression is the function sending F to P F 0.
correspondence.leanType: def unaryToMulti (P : Function Conf → Function Conf) : CalConf.MultiOperator 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Regard a rank-one multi-operator as an ordinary unary ambient operator. In explicit terms, for p of type cal conf.multi operator 1, its defining expression is the function sending f to P fun maps to f.
correspondence.leanType: def multiToUnary (P : CalConf.MultiOperator 1) : Function Conf → Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For P of type Function Conf maps to Function Conf, and F of type Cal Conf.Multi Input 1, Cal Conf.unary To Multi P F equals P F 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem unaryToMulti_apply (P : Function Conf → Function Conf) (F : CalConf.MultiInput 1) : CalConf.unaryToMulti P F = P (F 0)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For P of type Cal Conf.Multi Operator 1, and f of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.multi To Unary P f equals P fun maps to f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem multiToUnary_apply (P : CalConf.MultiOperator 1) (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.multiToUnary P f = P (fun _ => f)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Replace one input slot. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, f of type cal conf.multi input r, i of type fin r, and f of type function conf, its defining expression is Function.update F i f.
correspondence.leanType: def replace {r : ℕ} (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (i : Fin r) (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.MultiInput r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Apply the same unary operator to every input slot. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, p of type function conf maps to function conf, and f of type cal conf.multi input r, its defining expression is the function sending i to P F i.
correspondence.leanType: def mapUnary {r : ℕ} (P : Function Conf → Function Conf) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) : CalConf.MultiInput r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r in the natural numbers, F of type Cal Conf.Multi Input r, i of type Fin r, and f of type Function Conf, replace F i f i equals f.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r\colon \mathbb{N},\;F\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiInput} r,\;i\colon \operatorname{Fin} r,\;f\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\quad \operatorname{replace} F i f i = f
correspondence.leanType: theorem replace_same {r : ℕ} (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (i : Fin r) (f : Function Conf) : replace F i f i = f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r in the natural numbers, P of type Function Conf maps to Function Conf, F of type Cal Conf.Multi Input r, and i of type Fin r, map Unary P F i equals P F i.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r\colon \mathbb{N},\;P\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf} \longrightarrow \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\;F\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiInput} r,\;i\colon \operatorname{Fin} r,\quad \operatorname{mapUnary} P F i = P (F i)
correspondence.leanType: theorem mapUnary_apply {r : ℕ} (P : Function Conf → Function Conf) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (i : Fin r) : mapUnary P F i = P (F i)

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
